name: Summer Squash Saute
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim and slice small yellow squash and zucchini into 1/2-inch thick pieces.
2. In a small bowl, stir together minced garlic, chopped parsley, grated lemon zest, and salt.
3. Heat butter in a 12-inch skillet over high heat.
4. When foam subsides, add the squash to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until the edges are lightly colored but the squash is still crisp, about 3 minutes.
5. Stir in the parsley mixture and continue sautéing, stirring constantly, until the squash is tender when pierced with a fork, about 2 more minutes.
6. Serve hot. (72 calories per serving.)
